div.head:
No. 1437
Memorandum by the Deputy Director of the Office of Near Eastern Affairs (Kopper) to the Assistant Secretary of State for Congressional Relations (McFall)

opener.dateline: Washington, May 8, 1952.This memorandum was prepared by Awalt between May 5 and 8.

list.head: Subject:
list.item: Discontinuance of Saudi Arabian Trade Discrimination Against US Jewish Firms.

div.p: You will recall that Senator LehmanSenator Herbert Lehman of New York. and Congressmen JavitsCongressman Jacob Javits of New York. and CellerCongressman Emmanuel Celler of New York. have made inquiries of the Department on behalf of certain of their constituentsThe letters under reference here, along with correspondence from other Congressmen and various business firms, are in Department of State files 486A.1112, 486A.1116, and 486A.1117. regarding the Saudi Arabian ban on imports from US firms under Jewish control or ownership. Efforts by the Department and the Embassy in Jidda have recently been successful in causing the Saudi Arabian Government to discontinue this discriminatory practice. Reference Jidda Embassy telegram no. 629 dated April 30 (Tab 1).Not printed. (486A.1112/4–3052)

It is suggested that you might wish to inform the Senator and Congressmen that such is the case and letters have accordingly been drafted to them for your signature (Tabs 2, 3 and 4).None printed. . . .
